step_rationale: Immune response to oxidized LDL; marker of oxidative lipoprotein modification.
mechanism: Anti-oxLDL antibodies reflect the extent of lipoprotein oxidative modification, a key atherogenic signal in subendothelial lipid retention.

references.title: Oxidized LDL and anti-oxidized LDL antibodies in atherosclerosis - Novel insights and future directions in diagnosis and therapy<sup/>.
